title: Rye Dum Kebab
description: A peppery and gooey rye dum kebab that is a hearty weeknight dish perfect for Saudi Arabian cuisine. This kosher-friendly option makes for an easy, satisfying meal.

ingredients.name: Rye Bread
ingredients.notes: Select a hearty rye bread that can hold up to the spices and kebab fillings.

ingredients.name: Ground Lamb or Chicken Breast
ingredients.notes: For vegetarian option, use 100% plant-based ground meat or tofu instead of meat.

ingredients.name: Olive Oil
ingredients.notes: Use high-heat oil for searing the kebab fillings.

ingredients.name: Red Onion
ingredients.notes: Chopped finely to add a touch of sweetness and spiciness.

ingredients.name: Tomato Paste
ingredients.notes: For color and flavor, blend with garlic for extra depth.

ingredients.name: Red Pepper Flakes
ingredients.notes: To add a spicy kick to the dish.

ingredients.name: Garlic
ingredients.notes: Minced finely to release its aromatic compounds.

ingredients.name: Salt and Black Pepper
ingredients.notes: Season according to taste, as spices can vary in strength.

instructions: Slice the rye bread into rounds or shapes that will hold together well for the kebab.
Mix red onion, tomato paste, garlic, and red pepper flakes in a small bowl. Season with salt and pepper.
Spread the seasoning mixture evenly on each side of the rye bread slices.
Fold the sides of the bread to enclose the filling. Place seam-side down for stability.
Heat 2 tablespoons of olive oil in an oven-safe skillet over medium heat. Once hot, place the kebab rolls cut-side up and sear until golden brown on both sides (about 2-3 minutes per side).
Transfer the skewers to a baking sheet lined with parchment paper or foil for easy clean-up.

Bake in preheated oven for about 15-20 minutes, flipping once halfway through, until they are heated and nicely browned on the outside. The internal temperature should reach at least 74°C (165°F) when checked with a meat thermometer for safety and doneness.
Let cool slightly before serving. Serve hot as a hearty dinner accompaniment or side dish.
